incur.en: to experience something unpleasant, such as a debt or loss, as a result of your actions

ex.en: The company incurred huge losses last year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 작년에 막대한 손실을 입었다.

ex.en: Doctors said the disease was incurable.
ex.ko: 의사들은 그 병이 불치병이라고 말했다.

incurable.en: used to describe a habit or attitude that cannot be changed

ex.en: He's an incurable optimist.
ex.ko: 그는 뿌리 깊은 낙천주의자다.

indebted.en: feeling grateful for a favor or help someone has given you

ex.en: I am deeply indebted to my mentor for her guidance.
ex.ko: 나는 지도해 주신 은사님께 깊이 신세를 지고 있다.

ex.en: The company is heavily indebted to its bank.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 은행에 많은 빚을 지고 있다.

indent.en: to start a line of text further from the margin than the rest

ex.en: Indent the first line of each paragraph.
ex.ko: 각 문단의 첫 줄을 들여써라.

independently.en: without being helped or influenced by other people

ex.en: She decided to live independently after college.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대학 졸업 후 독립적으로 살기로 했다.

independently.en: separately from each other, without connection

ex.en: The two scientists discovered the result independently.
ex.ko: 그 두 과학자는 각자 따로 그 결과를 발견했다.

indescribable.ko: 형언할 수 없는, 이루 말할 수 없는
indescribable.en: too extreme or unusual to be described in words

ex.en: The view from the summit was indescribable.
ex.ko: 정상에서 본 경치는 말로 표현할 수 없었다.

index.en: a number that shows the level of something compared with earlier measurements

ex.en: The stock index rose sharply today.
ex.ko: 주가지수가 오늘 크게 올랐다.

index.en: an alphabetical list of names or subjects at the end of a book, showing which page they are on

ex.en: Check the index to find the page you need.
ex.ko: 필요한 페이지를 찾으려면 색인을 확인해라.

index.en: to make a list of information so it can be found and searched easily

ex.en: Search engines index millions of web pages.
ex.ko: 검색 엔진은 수백만 개의 웹페이지를 색인화한다.

indifferent.en: not interested in or caring about something

ex.en: He seemed indifferent to the news.
ex.ko: 그는 그 소식에 무관심해 보였다.

indifferent.en: neither very good nor very bad; mediocre

ex.en: The food at the restaurant was rather indifferent.
ex.ko: 그 식당의 음식은 다소 평범했다.

indignity.en: treatment that makes you feel embarrassed or disrespected

ex.en: She suffered the indignity of being searched at the airport.
ex.ko: 그녀는 공항에서 몸수색을 당하는 굴욕을 겪었다.

indispensable.en: so important that you cannot manage without it

ex.en: A good dictionary is indispensable for language learners.
ex.ko: 좋은 사전은 언어 학습자에게 필수적이다.

indulge.en: to allow yourself to enjoy something pleasurable, especially too much

ex.en: She indulged in a piece of chocolate cake.
ex.ko: 그녀는 초콜릿 케이크 한 조각을 마음껏 즐겼다.

indulge.en: to let someone have or do whatever they want

ex.en: Grandparents often indulge their grandchildren.
ex.ko: 조부모는 흔히 손주들의 응석을 받아준다.

indulgent.en: allowing someone to have or do whatever they want, often too easily

ex.en: Indulgent parents rarely say no to their children.
ex.ko: 지나치게 관대한 부모는 아이에게 좀처럼 안 된다고 말하지 않는다.

industrialize.en: to develop industries in a country or region on a large scale

ex.en: The country industrialized rapidly in the 20th century.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 20세기에 빠르게 산업화되었다.

ineffective.en: not achieving what it is supposed to achieve

ex.en: The new policy proved ineffective in reducing traffic.
ex.ko: 새 정책은 교통량을 줄이는 데 효과가 없는 것으로 드러났다.

inert.en: not able to move or react chemically

ex.en: Nitrogen is a relatively inert gas.
ex.ko: 질소는 비교적 비활성인 기체다.

inert.en: not moving or reacting; lacking energy

ex.en: He lay inert on the sofa all afternoon.
ex.ko: 그는 오후 내내 소파 위에서 무기력하게 누워 있었다.

inexperienced.en: lacking knowledge or skill in a particular activity

ex.en: The job is too difficult for an inexperienced worker.
ex.ko: 그 일은 경험이 없는 직원에게는 너무 어렵다.

infant.cue: 태어난 지 얼마 안 된 아기
infant.en: a baby or very young child

ex.en: The infant slept peacefully in her crib.
ex.ko: 그 유아는 아기 침대에서 평화롭게 잤다.

infect.en: to give someone a disease by spreading germs, bacteria, or a virus

ex.en: The wound became infected.
ex.ko: 그 상처는 감염되었다.

infect.en: to spread a feeling or attitude to other people quickly

ex.en: Her enthusiasm infected the whole team.
ex.ko: 그녀의 열정이 팀 전체에 전염되었다.

infected.en: having a disease caused by germs, bacteria, or a virus

ex.en: The cut on his hand looked infected.
ex.ko: 그의 손에 난 상처가 감염된 것처럼 보였다.

infectious.en: (of a disease) able to spread easily from one person to another

ex.en: Measles is a highly infectious disease.
ex.ko: 홍역은 전염성이 매우 강한 질병이다.

infectious.en: (of a feeling or attitude) spreading quickly to affect other people

ex.en: Her laughter was infectious.
ex.ko: 그녀의 웃음은 전염성이 있었다.

infer.en: to reach a conclusion based on evidence and reasoning rather than direct statement

ex.en: From her tone, I inferred that she was upset.
ex.ko: 그녀의 말투에서 나는 그녀가 화가 났다고 추론했다.

inference.en: a conclusion reached by reasoning from evidence

ex.en: We can draw a reasonable inference from these results.
ex.ko: 우리는 이 결과들로부터 합리적인 추론을 이끌어낼 수 있다.

inflammation.en: a red, swollen, and painful reaction in a part of the body caused by injury or infection

ex.en: The doctor prescribed medicine to reduce the inflammation.
ex.ko: 의사는 염증을 줄이기 위해 약을 처방했다.

inflammatory.en: relating to or causing inflammation in the body

ex.en: The cream helps with inflammatory skin conditions.
ex.ko: 그 크림은 염증성 피부 질환에 도움이 된다.

inflammatory.en: likely to cause anger or violence

ex.en: The politician made an inflammatory speech.
ex.ko: 그 정치인은 선동적인 연설을 했다.

inflate.en: to fill something such as a tire or balloon with air or gas

ex.en: He inflated the tires before the trip.
ex.ko: 그는 여행 전에 타이어에 바람을 넣었다.

inflate.en: to make something, such as a number or importance, seem larger than it really is

ex.en: The company was accused of inflating its profits.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 이익을 부풀렸다는 의혹을 받았다.

inflation.en: a general increase in prices over time

ex.en: Inflation has pushed up the cost of living.
ex.ko: 인플레이션으로 생활비가 올랐다.

influential.en: having a lot of power to affect people or events

ex.en: She is one of the most influential leaders in the industry.
ex.ko: 그녀는 업계에서 가장 영향력 있는 리더 중 한 명이다.

influenza.en: a serious infectious illness that causes fever, aching, and weakness, similar to a bad cold

ex.en: She was diagnosed with influenza and stayed home from work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 독감 진단을 받고 회사에 가지 않았다.

informal.en: relaxed and friendly, or not following strict rules

ex.en: It was an informal meeting between old friends.
ex.ko: 오랜 친구들 간의 격식 없는 만남이었다.

informal.en: (of language) used in everyday conversation rather than official writing

ex.en: 'Gonna' is an informal way of saying 'going to'.
ex.ko: 'gonna'는 'going to'의 구어체 표현이다.

infringe.en: to break a law or rule, or to intrude on someone's rights

ex.en: The company was sued for infringing the patent.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 특허를 침해했다는 이유로 소송을 당했다.

infringement.en: an act of breaking a law or violating someone's rights

ex.en: Copyright infringement can lead to legal action.
ex.ko: 저작권 침해는 법적 조치로 이어질 수 있다.

inhabit.en: to live in a particular place

ex.en: These forests are inhabited by rare species of birds.
ex.ko: 이 숲에는 희귀한 조류들이 서식한다.

inherit.en: to receive money, property, or a position from someone after they die or retire

ex.en: She inherited a large fortune from her grandmother.
ex.ko: 그녀는 할머니로부터 큰 재산을 상속받았다.

inherit.en: to have a characteristic that your parents or ancestors had, passed down genetically

ex.en: He inherited his father's brown eyes.
ex.ko: 그는 아버지의 갈색 눈을 물려받았다.

initiative.en: the ability to decide and act on your own without being told what to do

ex.en: She showed great initiative by starting the project herself.
ex.ko: 그녀는 직접 그 프로젝트를 시작하며 뛰어난 주도성을 보였다.

initiative.en: a new plan or process to solve a problem or improve a situation

ex.en: The government launched an initiative to reduce plastic waste.
ex.ko: 정부는 플라스틱 쓰레기를 줄이기 위한 계획을 시작했다.

injection.en: an act of putting liquid medicine into someone's body using a needle

ex.en: The nurse gave him an injection to ease the pain.
ex.ko: 간호사는 통증을 줄이기 위해 그에게 주사를 놓았다.

injection.en: an amount of money added to something to help it, or a sudden addition of something

ex.en: The company needs a cash injection to survive.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 살아남기 위해 자금 투입이 필요하다.

injured.en: hurt physically, especially in an accident

ex.en: Two passengers were injured in the crash.
ex.ko: 그 사고로 승객 두 명이 부상당했다.

ex.en: She spoke in an injured tone after the criticism.
ex.ko: 그녀는 비판을 듣고 마음이 상한 어조로 말했다.

innate.en: existing in a person or animal from birth, not learned

ex.en: Children have an innate curiosity about the world.
ex.ko: 아이들은 세상에 대한 타고난 호기심을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: He was innocently caught up in the scandal.
ex.ko: 그는 아무 잘못 없이 그 스캔들에 휘말렸다.

innocently.en: in a way that shows no bad intention or awareness of wrongdoing

ex.en: She innocently asked why everyone was staring.
ex.ko: 그녀는 왜 다들 쳐다보는지 순진하게 물었다.

innovation.en: the introduction of new ideas, methods, or products

ex.en: The company is known for its innovation in technology.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 기술 혁신으로 유명하다.

innovative.en: using new ideas or methods; original and creative

ex.en: They came up with an innovative solution to the problem.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 문제에 대한 혁신적인 해결책을 내놓았다.

input.en: information or data that is put into a computer or system

ex.en: The program requires user input to run.
ex.ko: 그 프로그램은 실행하려면 사용자 입력이 필요하다.

input.en: ideas, opinions, or information that you contribute to a discussion or decision

ex.en: We'd like your input on this proposal.
ex.ko: 이 제안에 대한 당신의 의견을 듣고 싶습니다.

ex.en: She inquired about the train schedule.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기차 시간표에 대해 문의했다.

inquire.en: to examine or investigate something carefully

ex.en: The committee will inquire into the accident.
ex.ko: 위원회는 그 사고를 조사할 것이다.

inseparable.en: so closely connected that two things or people cannot be separated

ex.en: The twins have been inseparable since childhood.
ex.ko: 그 쌍둥이는 어릴 때부터 떼려야 뗄 수 없는 사이다.

inspect.en: to look at something carefully to check its condition or quality

ex.en: The mechanic inspected the car before the trip.
ex.ko: 정비사는 여행 전에 차를 점검했다.

inspector.en: a police officer of a certain rank

ex.en: Inspector Lee led the investigation.
ex.ko: 이 경위가 수사를 지휘했다.

inspector.en: someone whose job is to check that things meet official standards

ex.en: A health inspector visited the restaurant.
ex.ko: 위생 검사관이 그 식당을 방문했다.

inspirational.en: making people feel encouraged or eager to do something

ex.en: Her story is truly inspirational.
ex.ko: 그녀의 이야기는 정말 영감을 준다.

ex.en: He recognized her instantly.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀를 즉시 알아보았다.

instill.en: to gradually make someone feel or believe something over time

ex.en: Parents try to instill good values in their children.
ex.ko: 부모는 아이들에게 좋은 가치관을 심어주려 한다.

instinct.en: a natural tendency to behave in a certain way without thinking or learning

ex.en: Animals rely on instinct to survive in the wild.
ex.ko: 동물은 야생에서 살아남기 위해 본능에 의존한다.

institution.en: a large organization, such as a university, bank, or hospital

ex.en: The university is one of the oldest institutions in the country.
ex.ko: 그 대학은 그 나라에서 가장 오래된 기관 중 하나다.

institution.en: a long-established custom or practice in a society

ex.en: Marriage is considered an important institution in many cultures.
ex.ko: 결혼은 많은 문화권에서 중요한 제도로 여겨진다.

instruct.en: to tell someone what to do, especially officially

ex.en: The manager instructed the staff to close early.
ex.ko: 매니저는 직원들에게 일찍 문을 닫으라고 지시했다.

instruct.en: to teach someone a subject or skill

ex.en: She instructs beginners in swimming.
ex.ko: 그녀는 초보자들에게 수영을 가르친다.

insufficient.en: not enough for a particular purpose

ex.en: There is insufficient evidence to prove his guilt.
ex.ko: 그의 유죄를 입증할 증거가 불충분하다.

insult.en: to say or do something that offends or is rude to someone

ex.en: He insulted her in front of everyone.
ex.ko: 그는 모든 사람 앞에서 그녀를 모욕했다.

insult.en: a rude remark or action intended to offend someone

ex.en: She ignored his insult and walked away.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 모욕을 무시하고 자리를 떠났다.

integrate.en: to combine two or more things so they work together as one

ex.en: The new software integrates well with existing systems.
ex.ko: 그 새 소프트웨어는 기존 시스템과 잘 통합된다.

integrate.en: to become part of a group or society and be fully accepted

ex.en: The school helps new students integrate into the community.
ex.ko: 그 학교는 새 학생들이 공동체에 통합되도록 돕는다.

intellectual.en: a person who enjoys studying and thinking deeply about ideas

ex.en: He was known as one of the great intellectuals of his time.
ex.ko: 그는 그 시대의 위대한 지식인 중 한 명으로 알려졌다.

intellectual.en: relating to the ability to think and understand ideas

ex.en: The book raises interesting intellectual questions.
ex.ko: 그 책은 흥미로운 지적 질문을 제기한다.

intent.en: giving all your attention to something

ex.en: She was intent on finishing the project by Friday.
ex.ko: 그녀는 금요일까지 그 프로젝트를 끝내는 데 몰두해 있었다.

intent.en: what someone plans or means to do

ex.en: He entered the building with criminal intent.
ex.ko: 그는 범죄 의도를 가지고 건물에 들어갔다.

interactive.en: allowing a two-way flow of information, especially between a computer and a user

ex.en: The museum has an interactive exhibit for children.
ex.ko: 그 박물관에는 어린이를 위한 쌍방향 전시가 있다.

interfere.en: to get involved in a situation without being wanted, often causing problems

ex.en: Please don't interfere in my personal affairs.
ex.ko: 제발 제 개인적인 일에 간섭하지 마세요.

interior.en: the inside part of something, such as a building or car

ex.en: The interior of the house was beautifully decorated.
ex.ko: 그 집의 내부는 아름답게 꾸며져 있었다.

interior.en: relating to or located on the inside of something

ex.en: She works as an interior designer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 실내 디자이너로 일한다.

interpersonal.en: relating to relationships or communication between people

ex.en: Good interpersonal skills are important in the workplace.
ex.ko: 직장에서는 좋은 대인관계 능력이 중요하다.

interpret.en: to explain or understand the meaning of something in a particular way

ex.en: She interpreted his silence as agreement.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 침묵을 동의로 해석했다.

interpret.en: to translate what someone is saying into another language, usually in real time

ex.en: He interpreted for the foreign visitors during the meeting.
ex.ko: 그는 회의 중에 외국인 방문객들을 위해 통역했다.

interpretation.en: a particular way of explaining or understanding something

ex.en: There are several interpretations of this poem.
ex.ko: 이 시에는 여러 해석이 있다.

interpreter.en: a person who translates spoken language from one language into another

ex.en: The interpreter translated the speech into English.
ex.ko: 그 통역사는 연설을 영어로 통역했다.

interruption.en: something that stops an activity or process for a period of time

ex.en: We continued the meeting without further interruption.
ex.ko: 우리는 더 이상의 방해 없이 회의를 계속했다.

intersection.en: a place where two or more roads meet or cross

ex.en: Turn left at the next intersection.
ex.ko: 다음 교차로에서 좌회전하세요.

intersection.cue: 서로 다른 것들이 공통으로 겹치는 부분
intersection.en: a point where two or more things overlap or have something in common

ex.en: The project sits at the intersection of art and technology.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 예술과 기술의 교집합에 위치한다.

intimate.en: having a very close personal relationship with someone

ex.en: They have been intimate friends for over twenty years.
ex.ko: 그들은 20년 넘게 친밀한 친구 사이다.

intimate.en: to suggest something indirectly without saying it plainly

ex.en: She intimated that she might resign soon.
ex.ko: 그녀는 곧 사임할지도 모른다고 넌지시 알렸다.

intransitive.en: (of a verb) not taking a direct object

ex.en: 'Arrive' is an intransitive verb.
ex.ko: 'arrive'는 자동사이다.

intravenous.en: given or done directly into a vein

ex.en: The patient was given intravenous fluids.
ex.ko: 환자는 정맥 주사로 수액을 맞았다.

intrude.en: to go into a place or situation where you are not wanted or invited

ex.en: I don't want to intrude on your family time.
ex.ko: 저는 당신의 가족 시간을 방해하고 싶지 않아요.

intruder.en: a person who enters a place without permission

ex.en: The alarm went off when an intruder entered the house.
ex.ko: 침입자가 집에 들어오자 경보가 울렸다.

intrusion.cue: 허락 없이 남의 영역에 들어오는 것
intrusion.en: an act of entering a place or situation without permission or welcome

ex.en: She felt his questions were an intrusion into her privacy.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 질문이 자신의 사생활에 대한 침해라고 느꼈다.

ex.en: His daily routine is almost invariable.
ex.ko: 그의 일상은 거의 변함이 없다.

ex.en: He invariably arrives late for meetings.
ex.ko: 그는 언제나 회의에 늦게 도착한다.

inventor.cue: 새로운 것을 처음 만들어 낸 사람
inventor.en: a person who creates or designs something new

ex.en: Thomas Edison was a famous inventor.
ex.ko: 토머스 에디슨은 유명한 발명가였다.

inverted commas.en: the punctuation marks used to show direct speech or a quotation

ex.en: Put the exact words in inverted commas.
ex.ko: 정확한 표현은 따옴표 안에 넣으세요.

investigate.en: to examine the facts of a situation carefully to find out the truth

ex.en: Police are investigating the cause of the fire.
ex.ko: 경찰은 화재의 원인을 조사하고 있다.

investigator.en: a police officer whose job is to examine crimes

ex.en: The investigator questioned several witnesses.
ex.ko: 그 수사관은 여러 목격자를 심문했다.

investigator.en: someone whose job is to examine and find out facts about something

ex.en: A team of investigators studied the plane crash.
ex.ko: 조사관 팀이 비행기 추락 사고를 조사했다.

investment.en: the act of putting money into something to make a profit

ex.en: Real estate can be a good investment.
ex.ko: 부동산은 좋은 투자가 될 수 있다.

investment.en: the time or effort you put into something to achieve a result

ex.en: Learning a language requires a big investment of time.
ex.ko: 언어를 배우는 데는 많은 시간 투자가 필요하다.

investor.en: a person who puts money into something to earn a profit

ex.en: Investors were nervous about the falling stock prices.
ex.ko: 투자자들은 하락하는 주가에 대해 불안해했다.

ex.en: Air is invisible but it is all around us.
ex.ko: 공기는 보이지 않지만 우리 주위에 가득하다.

invisible.cue: 잘 드러나지 않고 눈에 띄지 않는
invisible.en: not easily noticed, even if not literally hidden

ex.en: Her hard work often goes invisible to others.
ex.ko: 그녀의 노고는 흔히 남들 눈에 띄지 않는다.

invitingly.en: in a way that attracts or tempts you

ex.en: The pool looked invitingly cool on a hot day.
ex.ko: 더운 날 그 수영장은 매력적으로 시원해 보였다.

invoke.en: to make a feeling, memory, or image appear in someone's mind

ex.en: The old photos invoked memories of her childhood.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 사진들은 그녀의 어린 시절 기억을 불러일으켰다.

invoke.en: to mention a law, rule, or right as a reason for doing something

ex.en: The government invoked emergency powers during the crisis.
ex.ko: 정부는 위기 상황에서 비상 권한을 발동했다.

involvement.en: the fact of taking part in or being connected with something

ex.en: His involvement in the project was crucial to its success.
ex.ko: 그의 프로젝트 관여가 성공에 결정적이었다.

ironically.en: in a way that is the opposite of what you would expect

ex.en: Ironically, the fire station burned down.
ex.ko: 아이러니하게도 그 소방서가 불에 탔다.

ironically.en: in a way that says the opposite of what you really mean, often humorously

ex.en: "Great weather," he said ironically, as the rain poured down.
ex.ko: 비가 쏟아지는데 그는 "날씨 좋네"라고 비꼬듯이 말했다.

irrational.en: not based on clear thinking or reason

ex.en: He has an irrational fear of spiders.
ex.ko: 그는 거미에 대한 비합리적인 두려움이 있다.

irrational.en: (of a number) unable to be expressed as a simple fraction

ex.en: Pi is an irrational number.
ex.ko: 파이는 무리수이다.

irresistible.cue: 너무 매력적이거나 강해서 거부할 수 없는
irresistible.ko: 저항할 수 없는, 거부할 수 없는
irresistible.en: so attractive, tempting, or strong that you cannot resist it

ex.en: The smell of fresh bread was irresistible.
ex.ko: 갓 구운 빵 냄새는 거부할 수 없었다.

irresistibly.en: in a way that is impossible to resist

ex.en: She found herself irresistibly drawn to the mysterious stranger.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 신비로운 낯선 사람에게 거부할 수 없이 끌렸다.

irresponsible.en: not thinking about the possible bad results of your actions

ex.en: It was irresponsible of him to leave the children alone.
ex.ko: 아이들만 남겨두고 간 것은 무책임한 행동이었다.

irritated.en: feeling annoyed or angry about something

ex.en: She was irritated by the constant noise.
ex.ko: 그녀는 끊임없는 소음에 짜증이 났다.

ex.en: His habit of interrupting is irritating.
ex.ko: 말을 끊는 그의 습관은 짜증난다.

irritating.en: causing physical discomfort, such as itching or stinging

ex.en: The chemical had an irritating effect on her skin.
ex.ko: 그 화학물질은 그녀의 피부에 자극적인 영향을 주었다.

ex.en: He tried to hide his irritation at the delay.
ex.ko: 그는 지연에 대한 짜증을 감추려 했다.

irritation.en: a physical reaction such as redness or itching on the skin

ex.en: The new soap caused skin irritation.
ex.ko: 그 새 비누는 피부 자극을 일으켰다.

islander.en: a person who lives on an island

ex.en: The islanders depend mostly on fishing.
ex.ko: 그 섬 주민들은 주로 어업에 의존한다.

italicize.en: to print or write words in slanted letters for emphasis

ex.en: Book titles are usually italicized.
ex.ko: 책 제목은 보통 이탤릭체로 쓴다.

itch.en: to have an uncomfortable feeling on the skin that makes you want to scratch

ex.en: My arm itches where the mosquito bit me.
ex.ko: 모기에 물린 팔이 가렵다.

itch.en: to have a strong desire to do something

ex.en: She was itching to tell everyone the news.
ex.ko: 그녀는 모두에게 그 소식을 몹시 전하고 싶어했다.

jaw.cue: 입을 이루는 얼굴의 아래 뼈 부분
jaw.en: the bone structure that forms the mouth and holds the teeth

ex.en: He broke his jaw in the accident.
ex.ko: 그는 사고로 턱뼈가 부러졌다.

jaw.en: the part of a tool that opens and closes to grip something

ex.en: Place the metal between the jaws of the vice.
ex.ko: 금속을 바이스의 조 사이에 끼워 넣어라.

jealously.en: in a way that shows jealousy or envy

ex.en: He watched jealously as she danced with someone else.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀가 다른 사람과 춤추는 것을 질투하며 지켜보았다.

jealously.en: in a very protective way, not wanting to share or lose something

ex.en: She jealously guarded her private recipe.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 비밀 레시피를 철저히 지켰다.

jeopardize.en: to put something at risk of being harmed or lost

ex.en: One mistake could jeopardize the whole project.
ex.ko: 실수 하나가 프로젝트 전체를 위태롭게 할 수 있다.

jeweler.en: a person who makes or sells jewelry

ex.en: She took her ring to the jeweler to be resized.
ex.ko: 그녀는 반지 사이즈를 조정하러 보석상에 갔다.

journalism.en: the work of collecting and writing news for newspapers, magazines, TV, or the internet

ex.en: She studied journalism in college.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대학에서 저널리즘을 공부했다.

judo.en: a sport in which two people fight using skill and quick movement rather than strength

ex.en: He has a black belt in judo.
ex.ko: 그는 유도 검은띠를 가지고 있다.

junk food.en: food that is unhealthy but tastes good, often high in fat, sugar, or salt

ex.en: Eating too much junk food isn't good for your health.
ex.ko: 정크푸드를 너무 많이 먹으면 건강에 좋지 않다.

juror.cue: 재판에서 판단을 내리는 시민 중 한 명
juror.en: a member of a jury in a court trial

ex.en: Each juror had to give their opinion on the case.
ex.ko: 각 배심원은 그 사건에 대한 의견을 내야 했다.

jury.en: a group of citizens who decide whether someone is guilty in a court case

ex.en: The jury found him not guilty.
ex.ko: 배심원단은 그에게 무죄를 선고했다.

jury.en: a group of people who judge a competition

ex.en: The jury awarded first prize to a young director.
ex.ko: 심사위원단은 한 젊은 감독에게 대상을 수여했다.

justify.en: to give a good reason for something or show that it is right

ex.en: How do you justify spending so much money on this?
ex.ko: 이 일에 그렇게 많은 돈을 쓰는 것을 어떻게 정당화하겠어요?

justify.en: to adjust the spacing of text so that both edges of a line are straight

ex.en: Justify the text so both margins are even.
ex.ko: 양쪽 여백이 고르도록 텍스트를 정렬해라.

kangaroo.en: a large Australian animal that jumps using its strong back legs and carries its young in a pouch

ex.en: Kangaroos can jump very high and fast.
ex.ko: 캥거루는 매우 높고 빠르게 뛸 수 있다.

karaoke.en: an entertainment activity where people sing popular songs along with recorded music

ex.en: We went out for karaoke after dinner.
ex.ko: 우리는 저녁 식사 후 노래방에 갔다.

keen.en: eager or enthusiastic about doing something

ex.en: She's very keen to start her new job.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 일을 몹시 시작하고 싶어한다.

ex.en: He has a keen eye for detail.
ex.ko: 그는 세부 사항을 보는 예리한 눈을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: The knife has a keen edge.
ex.ko: 그 칼은 날이 매우 날카롭다.

keeper.en: a person whose job is to look after a place, building, or animals

ex.en: The zoo keeper fed the elephants every morning.
ex.ko: 그 동물원 사육사는 매일 아침 코끼리에게 먹이를 주었다.

kerosene.en: a type of oil used as fuel for heaters and lamps

ex.en: They used kerosene to light the old lamp.
ex.ko: 그들은 오래된 램프에 불을 붙이려고 등유를 사용했다.

keystroke.en: a single press of a key on a keyboard

ex.en: The software records every keystroke you make.
ex.ko: 그 소프트웨어는 사용자의 모든 키 입력을 기록한다.

kneel.en: to go down on your knees

ex.en: He knelt down to tie his shoelaces.
ex.ko: 그는 신발끈을 묶으려고 무릎을 꿇었다.

knot.en: a fastening made by tying a piece of rope, string, or thread

ex.en: She tied a knot in the rope.
ex.ko: 그녀는 밧줄에 매듭을 지었다.

knot.en: a unit of speed used for ships and aircraft, equal to one nautical mile per hour

ex.en: The ship was sailing at 20 knots.
ex.ko: 그 배는 20노트로 항해하고 있었다.

knowingly.en: in a way that shows you know what you are doing, often when doing something wrong

ex.en: He knowingly broke the rules.
ex.ko: 그는 알면서도 고의로 규칙을 어겼다.

ex.en: He is a well-known actor in Korea.
ex.ko: 그는 한국에서 잘 알려진 배우이다.

label.en: a piece of paper or material attached to something, giving information about it

ex.en: Check the label for the ingredients list.
ex.ko: 성분 목록을 확인하려면 라벨을 봐라.

label.en: a company that produces and sells music recordings

ex.en: She signed with a major record label.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대형 음반사와 계약했다.

label.en: to describe someone or something in a way that may be unfair, using a word or phrase

ex.en: The media labeled him a troublemaker.
ex.ko: 언론은 그를 말썽꾼이라고 낙인찍었다.

laborer.en: a person who does hard physical work, often outdoors

ex.en: The laborers worked all day building the road.
ex.ko: 그 노동자들은 하루 종일 도로를 건설했다.

ex.en: That's a pretty lame excuse for being late.
ex.ko: 지각한 것치고는 꽤 설득력 없는 변명이다.

lame.en: unable to walk normally because of an injury to the leg or foot

ex.en: The horse went lame after the race.
ex.ko: 그 말은 경주 후에 다리를 절게 되었다.

lance.en: a long weapon with a pointed metal head, used especially by knights on horseback

ex.en: The knight charged forward with his lance.
ex.ko: 그 기사는 창을 들고 돌진했다.

lance.en: to cut open a boil or swelling to let out pus

ex.en: The doctor lanced the infected boil.
ex.ko: 의사는 감염된 종기를 절개했다.

landing.en: the act of an aircraft coming down to the ground

ex.en: The plane made a smooth landing.
ex.ko: 그 비행기는 순조롭게 착륙했다.

landing.en: a flat area at the top of a set of stairs or between flights of stairs

ex.en: There was a small table on the landing.
ex.ko: 계단참에는 작은 탁자가 있었다.

landlady.en: a woman who rents a house, room, or apartment to tenants

ex.en: The landlady asked us to pay the rent on time.
ex.ko: 그 여자 집주인은 우리에게 제때 월세를 내라고 했다.

landslide.cue: 산비탈의 흙과 돌이 무너져 내리는 것
landslide.en: a sudden fall of a large amount of earth and rocks down a mountain or hill

ex.en: Heavy rain caused a landslide that blocked the road.
ex.ko: 폭우로 산사태가 나서 도로가 막혔다.

landslide.en: an overwhelming victory in an election

ex.en: She won the election in a landslide.
ex.ko: 그녀는 압승으로 선거에서 이겼다.

lap.en: the flat area formed by the upper legs of a person who is sitting down

ex.en: The cat curled up on her lap.
ex.ko: 고양이는 그녀의 무릎 위에서 몸을 웅크렸다.

lap.cue: 경주 트랙을 한 바퀴 도는 것
lap.en: one complete circuit of a racetrack or course

ex.en: He finished the last lap in record time.
ex.ko: 그는 마지막 바퀴를 기록적인 시간에 끝냈다.

lap.en: (of water) to move gently against something, making a soft sound

ex.en: Waves lapped against the shore.
ex.ko: 파도가 해안에 부드럽게 철썩였다.

laptop.en: a small portable computer that you can carry and use anywhere

ex.en: She types her reports on her laptop.
ex.ko: 그녀는 노트북으로 보고서를 작성한다.

lark.en: a small brown bird known for its beautiful song, often heard high in the sky

ex.en: A lark sang sweetly above the field.
ex.ko: 종달새가 들판 위에서 아름답게 지저귀었다.

lark.en: something done just for fun or excitement, often without serious purpose

ex.en: They dyed their hair blue just for a lark.
ex.ko: 그들은 그냥 재미 삼아 머리를 파란색으로 염색했다.

laser.en: a device that produces a powerful, narrow beam of light

ex.en: Doctors used a laser to correct her eyesight.
ex.ko: 의사들은 레이저를 사용해 그녀의 시력을 교정했다.

last minute.cue: 시간이 거의 다 된 마지막 시점
last minute.en: the very last moment before something must happen

ex.en: She always finishes her work at the last minute.
ex.ko: 그녀는 항상 마지막 순간에 일을 끝낸다.

last-minute.en: done or made just before a deadline

ex.en: We made some last-minute changes to the plan.
ex.ko: 우리는 계획에 막판 변경을 좀 했다.

lastly.en: used to introduce the final point in a list or speech

ex.en: Lastly, I would like to thank my family.
ex.ko: 마지막으로 제 가족에게 감사드리고 싶습니다.

laundry.en: clothes and other items that need to be washed or have just been washed

ex.en: I need to do the laundry this weekend.
ex.ko: 나는 이번 주말에 빨래를 해야 한다.

laundry.en: a place where clothes are washed and ironed

ex.en: He dropped off his shirts at the laundry.
ex.ko: 그는 세탁소에 셔츠를 맡겼다.

lava.en: hot melted rock that flows from a volcano

ex.en: Lava flowed down the side of the volcano.
ex.ko: 용암이 화산의 옆면을 따라 흘러내렸다.

lawmaker.en: a person who makes laws, such as a member of a government

ex.en: Lawmakers debated the new tax bill.
ex.ko: 국회의원들은 새 세금 법안에 대해 논쟁했다.

lawn.en: an area of short, cut grass in a garden or park

ex.en: He was mowing the lawn on Saturday morning.
ex.ko: 그는 토요일 아침에 잔디를 깎고 있었다.

lawsuit.en: a legal case brought by one person or group against another in court

ex.en: She filed a lawsuit against her former employer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 전 고용주를 상대로 소송을 제기했다.

layabout.en: a person who is lazy and avoids work

ex.en: His neighbors thought he was a layabout.
ex.ko: 그의 이웃들은 그를 게으름뱅이라고 생각했다.

leaflet.en: a printed sheet of paper giving information, often given out free

ex.en: A volunteer handed me a leaflet about recycling.
ex.ko: 한 자원봉사자가 나에게 재활용에 관한 전단지를 건네주었다.

leak.en: (of a liquid or gas) to escape through a hole or crack

ex.en: Water was leaking from the pipe.
ex.ko: 파이프에서 물이 새고 있었다.

leak.en: to deliberately give secret information to the public or media

ex.en: Someone leaked the documents to the press.
ex.ko: 누군가 그 문서를 언론에 유출했다.

learning.en: the process of gaining knowledge or skill through study or experience

ex.en: Learning a new language takes time and practice.
ex.ko: 새로운 언어를 학습하는 데는 시간과 연습이 필요하다.

learning.en: great knowledge gained from years of study

ex.en: He was a man of great learning.
ex.ko: 그는 학식이 깊은 사람이었다.

lease.en: a legal agreement allowing someone to use property for a period of time in exchange for payment

ex.en: They signed a two-year lease on the apartment.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 아파트에 대해 2년짜리 임대차 계약을 맺었다.

lease.en: to allow someone to use property or equipment in exchange for regular payments

ex.en: The company leases office space downtown.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 시내에 사무 공간을 임대한다.

leash.en: a rope or strap used to hold and control a dog or other animal

ex.en: Please keep your dog on a leash in the park.
ex.ko: 공원에서는 개를 목줄에 매어 두세요.

leash.en: to strictly control someone's freedom or actions

ex.en: The manager kept a tight leash on the team's spending.
ex.ko: 그 관리자는 팀의 지출을 엄격히 통제했다.

lecturer.en: a person who gives talks to teach a subject, especially at a university

ex.en: She works as a lecturer in the biology department.
ex.ko: 그녀는 생물학과 강사로 일한다.

leek.en: a long vegetable with a white base and green leaves, related to the onion

ex.en: She added leeks to the soup.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수프에 리크를 넣었다.

legend.en: an old story about famous people or events that may not be entirely true

ex.en: According to legend, a dragon once lived in these mountains.
ex.ko: 전설에 따르면 예전에 이 산에 용이 살았다고 한다.

legend.en: a list explaining the symbols used on a map or chart

ex.en: Check the legend to understand what each symbol means.
ex.ko: 각 기호가 무엇을 뜻하는지 알려면 범례를 확인해라.

legendary.en: extremely famous and admired, almost like a legend

ex.en: He was a legendary basketball player.
ex.ko: 그는 전설적인 농구 선수였다.

legislate.en: to make a law about something

ex.en: The government plans to legislate against plastic bags.
ex.ko: 정부는 비닐봉지를 규제하는 법을 제정할 계획이다.

legislation.en: a law or set of laws made by a government

ex.en: New legislation was passed to protect the environment.
ex.ko: 환경을 보호하기 위한 새 법률이 통과되었다.

legislative.en: relating to the process of making laws

ex.en: Congress is the legislative branch of the US government.
ex.ko: 의회는 미국 정부의 입법 기관이다.

legislator.en: a person who makes laws, such as a member of a parliament or congress

ex.en: State legislators voted on the new bill.
ex.ko: 주 의회 의원들은 새 법안에 투표했다.

legislature.en: the group of people in a country or state who have the power to make laws

ex.en: The state legislature approved the budget.
ex.ko: 주 의회는 예산안을 승인했다.

ex.en: He runs a legitimate business.
ex.ko: 그는 합법적인 사업체를 운영한다.

legitimate.en: reasonable and fair, or based on sound reasoning

ex.en: That's a legitimate concern about the plan.
ex.ko: 그것은 그 계획에 대한 정당한 우려다.

leisurely.en: in an unhurried and relaxed way

ex.en: We walked leisurely along the beach.
ex.ko: 우리는 해변을 따라 여유롭게 걸었다.

ex.en: They enjoyed a leisurely breakfast on Sunday.
ex.ko: 그들은 일요일에 느긋한 아침 식사를 즐겼다.
